title: myosotis arvensis subsp. sylvatica
intro: Myosotis arvensis subsp. sylvatica, also known as woodland forget-me-not, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of woodland forget-me-not include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat coughs, colds, and skin issues.
